articles.title: Odisha Swelters Under Scorching Heatwave
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Odisha
articles.full_text:
Odisha Swelters Under Scorching Heatwave
Odisha faced a heatwave with temperatures soaring past 40°C in several cities. Jharsuguda recorded the highest at 41.6°C, while Bhubaneswar saw 38.7°C. A slight temperature dip is expected over the weekend, with thunderstorms likely in some districts, according to the IMD bulletin.
Odisha is reeling under a severe heatwave as the mercury climbed above 40 degrees Celsius in nine places on Friday. Jharsuguda topped the list with a scorching 41.6 degrees, according to the Indian Meteorological Department (IMD).
Angul reported a high of 40.7 degrees, closely followed by Titlagarh and Bhadrak at 40.5 degrees Celsius. Other cities like Hirakud and Bolangir also witnessed temperatures surpassing 40 degrees, a challenging ordeal for residents.
Though Bhubaneswar and Cuttack experienced slightly milder temperatures of 38.7 and 38.4 degrees Celsius respectively, relief is on the horizon. IMD forecasts predict a gradual dip in temperatures over the weekend with thunderstorms in some southern districts.
(With inputs from agencies.)
- Hirakud

articles.title: Heatwave Hits Odisha: Several Districts Cross 40°C - Hindusthan Samachar English
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar, 03 April (H.S.): Odisha is grappling with intense heatwave conditions, with temperatures soaring across several districts, particularly in the western region of the state. Several areas have recorded temperatures above 40°C, highlighting the severity of the ongoing heatwave.
Jharsuguda emerged as the hottest location in Odisha, registering a maximum temperature of 40.6°C. Other major hot spots include Titlagarh at 40°C, and Hirakud and Sonepur at 39.2°C each. Angul recorded 39.1°C, while Bhadrak and Bolangir reported 39°C. Most other cities across the state experienced temperatures above 38°C, indicating widespread heatwave conditions.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued warnings for districts including Nuapada, Titlagarh, Bolangir, Sonepur, Boudh, Kalahandi, Jharsuguda, Sundargarh, and Rourkela, where temperatures are expected to range between 40°C and 42°C in the coming days.
Hot and humid conditions persist across interior Odisha, increasing discomfort for residents, while coastal regions continue to experience relatively milder weather, creating a noticeable temperature divide across the state.
Authorities have advised people to take necessary precautions, stay hydrated, and avoid prolonged exposure to the sun as the heatwave is expected to persist in the western districts.
---------------
Hindusthan Samachar / Monalisa Panda

articles.title: Odisha Sizzles As Jharsuguda Tops 41.6°C; Heat Intensifies Across State - Ommcom News
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar: The India Meteorological Department’s Meteorological Centre in Bhubaneswar on Friday reported a sharp rise in temperatures across Odisha, with several districts experiencing intense heat during the day.
According to the evening weather bulletin issued at 7:00 PM, western and northern parts of the state bore the brunt of the heatwave-like conditions. Jharsuguda emerged as the hottest place at 41.6°C, followed closely by Sambalpur (40.0°C), Sundargarh (39.5°C), and Rourkela (39.5°C).
In coastal regions, Chandbali recorded 39.3°C, while Balasore and Bhadrak reported temperatures above 38°C, indicating rising discomfort even in typically moderate zones. The state capital Bhubaneswar registered 38.7°C, remaining significantly above normal levels.
Among other districts, Angul (40.7°C), Bargarh (38.0°C), and Titlagarh (40.5°C) also witnessed soaring temperatures. Meanwhile, relatively lower maximum temperatures were recorded in Koraput (33.8°C) and Rayagada (33.6°C), offering slight relief in southern parts.
Humidity levels remained moderately high, particularly in coastal and southern districts, contributing to increased discomfort. Rainfall activity was largely negligible across the state, with only isolated traces reported in a few areas.
The IMD has indicated that such high temperatures may persist over the coming days, urging residents to take precautions against heat-related illnesses, especially during peak afternoon hours.
